items.title: Fix/webchat session isolation
items.content:
Summary

    Problem: When multiple agents share webchat via Control UI (separate browser tabs), broadcast("chat", payload) sends chat events (delta streaming, final messages) to ALL WebSocket clients instead of only connections subscribed to the originating session.
    Why it matters: Causes cross-agent message leakage. Agent A's responses appear in Agent B's webchat tab. With agentToAgent enabled, this creates spam loops where agents see and react to each other's messages.
    What changed: Wired existing SessionMessageSubscriberRegistry (already tracks per-session WS subscriptions) into chat event broadcasting. Replaced broadcast("chat", ...) with broadcastToConnIds() targeting session subscribers, falling back to global broadcast when no subscribers exist.
    What did NOT change (scope boundary): External channel delivery (Telegram, Feishu, Slack) untouched. No config changes. No API changes. No changes to session binding, routing, or agent-to-agent messaging logic.

Change Type (select all)

    Bug fix
    Feature
    Refactor required for the fix
    Docs
    Security hardening
    Chore/infra

Scope (select all touched areas)

    Gateway / orchestration
    Skills / tool execution
    Auth / tokens
    Memory / storage
    Integrations
    API / contracts
    UI / DX
    CI/CD / infra

Linked Issue/PR

    Closes #51812
    Related #47745
    This PR fixes a bug or regression

Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

    Root cause: Chat events in server-chat.ts (emitChatFinal, emitChatDelta) and server-methods/chat.ts (broadcastChatFinal, broadcastChatError) used broadcast("chat", ...) which sends to every connected WebSocket client. The SessionMessageSubscriberRegistry infrastructure existed (used for transcript updates and tool events) but was never wired into chat event delivery.
    Missing detection / guardrail: No test covered the multi-agent webchat isolation scenario.
    Prior context: Issue #47745 ad

## Summary

Implements D20 and D21 from the Week 6 backlog: structured persona sections for IR v0.2.

## D20: IR model + parsers

- Added `sections: dict[str, str] | None` field to `Persona` model (`ir.py`)
- Created `src/agentshift/sections.py` with `extract_sections()` utility:
  - H2-first with H3 fallback algorithm (spec §3.1)
  - Full alias normalization via `ALIAS_MAP` (spec Appendix B)
  - Duplicate slug merging with debug-level warning
- Updated **OpenClaw parser** to call `extract_sections()` and populate `persona.sections`
- Updated **Claude Code parser** similarly
- Updated `specs/ir-schema.json` to add `sections` property to the `persona` object

## D21: Emitters + diff

### Bedrock emitter
- `_build_instruction()` assembles instruction from sections (overview, behavior, tools, knowledge, persona) per spec §5.1
- `_build_guardrail_config()` extracts `guardrails` section → Bedrock `topicPolicyConfig`
- Writes `guardrail-config.json` when guardrails section is present
- CloudFormation template includes TODO stub for guardrail resource reference
- Fallback: when `sections` is absent, uses `system_prompt` (unchanged behavior)

### Vertex emitter
- `_build_goal()` uses `sections['overview']` as the goal field (spec §5.2)
- `_build_structured_instructions()` assembles behavior/persona/tools/knowledge with guardrails as 'Restrictions:' block
- Fallback: when `sections` is absent, existing `_extract_instructions()` used

### diff.py
- `SECTION_PLATFORM_SUPPORT` map for per-section coverage tracking
- `persona_sections` component added to portability matrix (informational, weight=0)
- `_count_mapped_sections()` for N/M mapped display per target
- `diff_agents()` for agent-to-agent section diff (Mode B, spec §6.3)
- `render_agent_diff_table()` with `--section` filter support

## Tests

All **473 existing tests pass** with no regressions. T13 (tester task) will add dedicated tests for persona.sections behavior.

## Spec
Implements [A11 spec](specs/persona-sections-

items.author: kenans

items.title: feat: configurable User-Agent for Anthropic API requests
items.content:
## 背景 / Background

Anthropic 在高峰期会降低非官方客户端的请求优先级。OpenClaw 默认发送 `User-Agent: openclaw`，会被识别为第三方客户端。

Anthropic deprioritises requests from unrecognised clients during peak hours. OpenClaw currently sends `User-Agent: openclaw`, which is identified as a third-party client.

## 改动 / Changes

### 核心逻辑
- **`src/agents/provider-attribution.ts`**
  - 新增 `DEFAULT_ANTHROPIC_USER_AGENT = 'claude-cli/2.1.80 (external, cli)'`
  - 新增 `resolveAnthropicUserAgent()` — 优先读 `OPENCLAW_ANTHROPIC_USER_AGENT` 环境变量，fallback 到默认值
  - `buildAnthropicAttributionPolicy()` — Anthropic attribution 策略改为 `enabledByDefault: true`，hook 从 `default-headers` 升级为 `request-headers`

- **`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/anthropic-stream-wrappers.ts`**
  - 新增 `createAnthropicAttributionHeadersWrapper()` — 为直连 Anthropic API（非 Bedrock/Vertex）的请求注入 User-Agent header，对标 OpenAI 的 `createOpenAIAttributionHeadersWrapper`

- **`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts`**
  - `applyPrePluginStreamWrappers()` 中当 `provider === 'anthropic'` 时调用上述 wrapper

- **`src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.claude.ts`**
  - OAuth 用量查询接口也使用 `resolveAnthropicUserAgent()` 替代硬编码的 `'openclaw'`

### CI
- **`.github/workflows/build.yml`** — 在 `self-hosted` runner 上运行：
  `npm install → npm run build → npm pack → upload *.tgz artifact`
  PR merge 后自动构建，artifact 可直接 `npm install -g` 安装

## 配置 / Configuration

默认无需配置，开箱即用。如需自定义 User-Agent：

```bash
export OPENCLAW_ANTHROPIC_USER_AGENT='claude-cli/2.1.80 (external, cli)'
```

## 影响范围 / Scope

- ✅ 仅影响直连 Anthropic API（`api === 'anthropic-messages'` && `provider === 'anthropic'` && public base URL）
- ✅ Bedrock / Vertex 不受影响
- ✅ 其他 provider（OpenAI、Google 等）不受影响
items.author: ytangguo

items.title: Improve Feishu streaming visibility and filter scratchpad text
items.content:
# Upstream Draft: Feishu IM Long-Task Visibility

## Issue Draft

### Title

Feishu IM long-task visibility: status footer, last activity, completion confirmation, and scratchpad suppression

### Summary

When OpenClaw is used through Feishu IM, long-running agent tasks can feel like a black box:

- users cannot tell whether the agent is still alive or stuck
- streaming text updates can pollute the message body
- panel completion and IM delivery can get out of sync
- internal Codex/OpenAI scratchpad text may leak into the chat

I prototyped a Feishu-specific improvement locally and the result feels much better for real long-running agent work.

The key idea is:

- keep the chat body for real output
- move task state into a card footer / note
- show low-noise `last activity`
- separate execution completion from IM delivery completion
- suppress internal scratchpad-style planning text from user-facing IM replies

### Problems Observed

#### 1. Long tasks feel like a black box

In Feishu, users often cannot tell whether the agent is:

- still working
- stuck
- disconnected
- already finished but failed to notify clearly

#### 2. Progress updates in the body are noisy

If heartbeat/progress is appended into the body, the result becomes chat pollution very quickly.

This is especially bad in IM interfaces where users expect concise, conversational updates.

#### 3. Completion and delivery are different states

Sometimes the panel/runtime shows the task as finished, but the Feishu side still looks stuck or never gives a clear completion signal.

#### 4. Internal scratchpad text can leak into Feishu

With Codex/OpenAI-style execution, partial English planning text may appear in the IM thread, for example:

- `Need maybe use direct curl...`
- `Let's inspect...`
- `Could create draft page...`

These are internal planning notes, not user-facing output.

## Proposed Design

### A. Add a status footer / note to Feishu streaming cards

Instead of appending progress text into the

items.title: feat(A11): persona.sections schema spec for IR v0.2
items.content:
## Summary

Adds `specs/persona-sections-schema.md` — the full spec for structured persona sections in IR v0.2.

Closes #3

## What this spec covers

1. **JSON Schema extension** — optional `persona.sections` map added to `ir-schema.json`; `ir_version` stays `"1.0"` (non-breaking)
2. **Parser algorithm** — H2/H3 heading detection, slug normalization, alias resolution, preamble handling
3. **Well-known section names** — 10 canonical slugs (`overview`, `behavior`, `guardrails`, `tools`, `knowledge`, `persona`, `examples`, `triggers`, `output-format`, `auth`) with full alias tables
4. **Platform emitter mappings**:
   - Bedrock: `guardrails` section → `guardrailConfiguration` (topic/content policy); other sections fill the 4k instruction budget
   - Vertex AI: `overview` → `goal`; `behavior` + others → `instructions`
   - Copilot: structured section assembly into `instructions` with optional `conversation_starters` from `examples`
   - OpenClaw/Claude Code: reconstruct body with proper H2 headings from sections map
5. **`agentshift diff` enhancements** — new "Persona Sections" row in portability matrix + agent-to-agent section diff mode (`agentshift diff v1.json v2.json`)
6. **Backward compatibility** — `sections` is always optional; `system_prompt` stays canonical; emitters degrade gracefully
7. **D20 / T13 handoff** — Python model stub, `extract_sections()` function signature, full test checklist

## Files changed
- `specs/persona-sections-schema.md` (new)
- `BACKLOG.md` — A11 status → pr-created

## Reviewers
@dev: D20 and D21 can be implemented against this spec
@tester: T13 test checklist is in §9

items.title: Node browser proxy: Chrome MCP existing-session (user profile) fails with Connection closed — consent dialog never appears
items.content:
## Summary

When using the node browser proxy to access a Chrome MCP existing-session (`user` profile) on a remote node, the connection fails with `McpError: MCP error -32000: Connection closed`. Chrome never shows the consent dialog that would authorize the connection.

The same `user` profile works perfectly when accessed via the **local** gateway on the same machine.

## Environment

- **Gateway:** OpenClaw 2026.3.24 on Ubuntu (AWS Lightsail VPS), bind=tailnet
- **Node:** OpenClaw 2026.3.24 on Ubuntu Desktop (marks-IT13), connected via Tailscale
- **Chrome:** 146.0.7680.164 (stable, not snap)
- **Chrome remote debugging:** Enabled via `chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging`
- **Display:** Wayland (Ubuntu desktop)

## Steps to reproduce

1. Configure gateway with `gateway.bind=tailnet`
2. Connect a Linux desktop as a node host: `openclaw node run --host <gateway-tailscale-ip> --port 18789`
3. Node connects successfully, shows `browser` capability, `browser.proxy` command available
4. From gateway, invoke: `openclaw nodes invoke --node <name> --command browser.proxy --params '{"path":"/tabs","query":{"profile":"user"},"method":"GET"}'`

## Expected

- The node host's browser service spawns `chrome-devtools-mcp --autoConnect`
- Chrome shows the consent/approval dialog on the desktop
- User accepts, connection established
- Gateway receives tab list via node proxy

## Actual

- Node browser proxy attempts Chrome MCP existing-session attach
- Fails immediately with: `BrowserProfileUnavailableError: Chrome MCP existing-session attach failed for profile "user". Details: McpError: MCP error -32000: Connection closed`
- Chrome **never shows** the consent dialog
- The managed `openclaw` profile works fine through the same node proxy

## Key observations

1. **Local gateway on the same machine works:** `openclaw browser --browser-profile user tabs` returns tabs correctly after consent is accepted
2. **Manual MCP trigger works:** Piping a JSON-RPC command directly into `npx chr

items.title: Feature Request: MCP Server for DeerFlow Backend to Enable Agent Tool Integration
items.content:
## Summary

Add an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server to the DeerFlow backend, enabling other AI agents and tools (like OpenClaw) to connect, scan tasks, assign work, and retrieve results from a running DeerFlow server.

## Motivation

DeerFlow is a powerful workflow orchestration engine, but it currently lacks a standardized way for other AI agents to interact with it programmatically. Adding MCP server support would allow:

- **OpenClaw** and similar agents to scan, assign, and retrieve tasks from DeerFlow
- **Multi-agent collaboration** where different AI tools can tap into DeerFlow workflows
- **Standardized integration** via the MCP protocol instead of custom REST/WebSocket APIs

## Proposed Solution

Implement an MCP server that wraps the DeerFlow backend, exposing key operations such as:

1. **Task Scanning** — List available workflows/tasks
2. **Task Assignment** — Submit new tasks or trigger workflow runs
3. **Result Retrieval** — Fetch outputs, logs, and status of completed tasks
4. **Session Management** — Create/manage DeerFlow execution sessions

### Example Use Cases

- An agent identifies a complex task, creates a DeerFlow workflow, and assigns it
- OpenClaw monitors a DeerFlow server, picks up completed results, and continues execution
- Multiple agents collaborate on a shared DeerFlow-backed pipeline

## Alternatives Considered

- Custom REST API (less standardized, more maintenance burden)
- Direct database integration (brittle, version-dependent)
- WebSocket-only pub/sub (doesn't support tool/agent discovery patterns)

MCP provides the best balance of standardization, discoverability, and bidirectional communication.

## Additional Context

See [MCP documentation](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) for the protocol spec. This would position DeerFlow as a first-class participant in the emerging multi-agent tooling ecosystem.

---\n_Requested by: OpenClaw user_\n_Use case: Enable OpenClaw ↔ DeerFlow task collaboration via MCP_

*   **底层架构分化**：主流项目普遍在向 **Rust 重构**（以换取极致性能与内存安全）或**运行时解耦**（以提升多模型适配性）迈进，这导致近期各项目频繁出现功能回退和不稳定迹象。
*   **应用层“硬化”**：生态焦点已从单纯的“对话”转向“执行”。**安全沙箱**、**多租户隔离**、**金融级执行层**以及**操作系统级交互**成为头部项目竞相攀爬的技术高地。
*   **连接协议之争**：随着 OpenClaw 插件标准的普及，各大项目（NanoBot, IronClaw, NullClaw）正密集跟进对**MCP (Model Context Protocol)** 和 **`.well-known` 技能分发标准**的支持，试图在未来的 Agent 互操作生态中占据一席之地。

items.title: feat(mcp-cli): add MCPorter wrapper for direct MCP tool invocation
items.content:
## Summary

- Adds `scripts/mcp_cli/` module wrapping `npx mcporter call` for direct MCP tool invocation from hooks and scripts without LLM inference overhead
- Replaces the need for hand-rolled JSON-RPC clients (like the 287-line `mcp_client.py`) with a thin subprocess wrapper
- Validated end-to-end: MCPorter 0.7.3 auto-discovers all 3 configured servers (Serena 20 tools, DeepWiki 3 tools, Forgetful 3 tools) from `.mcp.json`

## Motivation

Every MCP tool invocation currently round-trips through the LLM, even for mechanical operations (memory writes, label updates). This wrapper enables deterministic operations to bypass the LLM entirely, saving tokens and enabling composability outside the agent loop.

## What changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `scripts/mcp_cli/__init__.py` | Package init with public API |
| `scripts/mcp_cli/wrapper.py` | `mcp_call()` and `mcp_list_tools()` functions wrapping MCPorter CLI |
| `tests/test_mcp_cli_wrapper.py` | 17 unit tests covering all paths |
| `pyproject.toml` | Added `scripts.mcp_cli*` to package discovery |

## Usage

```python
from scripts.mcp_cli import mcp_call

# Call Serena memory tools directly
result = mcp_call("serena", "list_memories")
result = mcp_call("serena", "read_memory", name="my-memory")

# Call any configured MCP server
result = mcp_call("forgetful", "discover_forgetful_tools")
result = mcp_call("deepwiki", "read_wiki_structure", repo="owner/repo")
```

## Test plan

- [x] 17 unit tests pass (pytest)
- [x] ruff lint clean
- [x] mypy type check clean
- [x] Manual validation: `npx mcporter call serena.list_memories` returns expected results

Fixes #1484

items.title: Evolution to Universal Agent Infrastructure (2026-07-09)
items.content:
This evolution advances MCP Any into the core infrastructure layer for autonomous agent swarms. It addresses critical scaling bottlenecks identified in Claude Code (Mailbox Lock Exhaustion) by introducing Interrupt-Aware Mailbox Sharding (IAMS) and counters emerging "Shadow-Context" vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-94001) with the Autonomous Swarm Kill-Switch (ASKS). Additionally, it integrates the OpenClaw v2.0 Mission-Root protocol for hardware-attested session resumption and establishes the architecture for a Zero-Trust Agent Identity Hub.

## 1. 今日速览

过去24小时内，OpenClaw 社区保持高度活跃，共产生 **500条 Issues 更新**（新开/活跃 444 条，关闭 56 条）和 **500条 PR 更新**（待合并 324 条，已合并/关闭 176 条），显示出强劲的开发与问题反馈节奏。尽管无新版本发布，但多个关键回归 Bug 被识别并已有修复 PR 提交，表明团队正集中应对近期版本（如 v2026.3.12–v2026.3.24）引入的稳定性问题。社区对多平台支持、工具调用可靠性及身份认证机制的讨论持续升温。

---

今日多个重要 PR 被合并或推进，显著提升系统稳定性与功能完整性：

- **#55967（已关闭）→ #56098（新开）**：为插件系统添加 `api.runtime.agent.abort` 接口，解决子代理无法主动终止运行的问题，增强多代理协作控制能力（[PR #56098](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56098)）。
- **#56083**：修复 `messages.responsePrefix` 未应用于 cron 消息的问题，确保定时任务输出格式一致性（[PR #56083](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56083)）。
- **#56092**：修正系统事件触发的心跳消息错误路由至当前会话而非配置会话，避免干扰用户对话（[PR #56092](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56092)）。
- **#56013**：扩展 Android 设备配对引导配置，支持 `operator` 角色与子集匹配验证，改善移动端接入体验（[PR #56013](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56013)）。
- **#55764**：新增本地 CLI TTS 插件，支持多代理独立语音合成配置，拓展无障碍与语音交互场景（[PR #55764](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/55764)）。

> 整体来看，项目在**多代理协同、消息路由一致性、设备接入稳定性**三个方向取得实质性进展。

items.author: github-actions[bot]

items.title: fix: stop leaking error.message in events API (L-005)
items.content:
## Security Audit Finding F-28-01

**Severity:** HIGH  
**Lesson violated:** L-005 (never return raw error.message to clients)

### Changes
- `/api/v1/events`: catch block returned `error.message` directly → now returns static `'Unable to fetch events.'` and logs the real error server-side
- `/api/v1/events/refresh`: catch block returned `error.message` → now returns static `'Failed to launch refresh.'`; also removed `userError?.message` from session_lookup_failed response (was leaking Supabase auth error details)

### Why this matters
Raw error messages can contain DB schema info, table names, constraint names, or internal details. Attackers use this for reconnaissance.

Found by nightly security audit (PRD-062), 2026-03-28.

items.title: feat: plugin tool consolidation — all phases (FR-1 through FR-6)
items.content:
## Summary

### Phase 1: Server — FR-1 (Remove 7 Redundant Tools)
- Remove 7 tools from `tools/list` (68 → 61). Dispatch aliases retained for backward compat.
- Removed: get_context_timeline, get_timeline_by_query, get_recent_context, find_by_file_context, get_observation_relationships, get_graph_neighbors, doc_update

### Phase 2: OpenClaw — FR-2 (Bug Fixes)
- `engram_decisions` now uses `/api/decisions/search` (was searchContext + client-side filter)
- `memory_forget` defaults to suppress (reversible) instead of archive. `permanent=true` for archive.
- Server: add `suppress` action to bulk-status endpoint
- Bump openclaw-engram to 2.0.9

### Phase 3: OpenClaw — FR-3 (Expand Tools: 8 → 17)
- 9 new tools: engram_rate, engram_suppress, engram_outcome, engram_find_by_file, engram_timeline, engram_changes, engram_how_it_works, engram_vault_store, engram_vault_get
- All tool descriptions include trigger conditions (NFR-3)
- New client methods: rateObservation, setSessionOutcome, getFileContext, getTimeline, storeCredential, getCredential
- Bump openclaw-engram to 2.0.10

### Phase 5: CC Plugin — FR-5+FR-6
- stop.js: Replace injected-observations API with single retrospective API call
- statusline.js: Add effectiveness metric with 60s cache (shows "eff:72%" or "eff:--")

### Deferred: Phase 4 (OpenClaw lifecycle hooks — FR-4)
- session_end outcome detection, utility tracking, before_tool_call file context
- Requires more complex heuristic design — separate PR

## Test plan
- [ ] `go build ./...` passes
- [ ] `go test ./internal/mcp/...` passes
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes for openclaw-engram
- [ ] tools/list returns 61 tools (was 68)
- [ ] Removed tool names still work via dispatch
- [ ] New OpenClaw tools compile and are registered (17 total)
- [ ] Statusline shows effectiveness metric

items.title: Add lazyazure and Money Burned CLI from approved submissions
items.content:
Two open issues with all automated triage checks passing (`repo-verified`, `has-license`, `has-readme`, `has-image`) were assessed at >90% confidence and added directly to the site content.

## Tools added

- **[lazyazure](https://github.com/matsest/lazyazure)** — Go TUI for browsing Azure subscriptions, resource groups, and resources from the terminal; inspired by lazydocker. Theme: `terminal`.
- **[Money Burned CLI](https://github.com/Money-Burned/mb-dotnet-console)** — C# CLI that tracks meeting cost as a real-time counter (stopwatch for money). Runs on Linux and Windows.

## Notes

Both files follow the same frontmatter schema and processing logic as the `batch-approve.yml` workflow (tag normalization, slug derivation, language/theme fields). Thumbnails are not included — the `refresh-thumbnails` workflow can populate those on next run.

Adding the `approved` label directly to the source issues was not possible in this environment (token lacks write access to issues). A maintainer should close issues #287 and #288 once this merges.

items.title: Outline: What Are AI Agents? A Developer's Guide
items.content:
## Article 3 — Sprint 1 (Writer 2)

**Type:** Explainer | **Area:** AI Agents | **Target date:** 2026-04-10

### Target Audience
Developers (junior to senior) comfortable with code but new to AI agents. May have used chatbots or coding assistants.

### Scope
Comprehensive explainer giving developers a working mental model of AI agents. Not a tutorial — practical and opinionated, not academic. Cross-references Article 5 (Sprint 2) for the hands-on follow-up.

### Key Sections
1. Definition & mental model (agents = LLM + tools + a loop)
2. Core components (reasoning engine, tools, memory, orchestration loop)
3. Agents vs chatbots vs automation scripts
4. Concrete walkthrough (flight search example)
5. Common architectures (ReAct, plan-then-execute, multi-agent, human-in-the-loop)
6. Honest strengths and limitations assessment
7. Getting started pointers for developers
8. Landscape outlook (MCP, A2A, ACP, infra trends)

### Open Questions
- **Code examples:** Thinking 1-2 short snippets to ground concepts, not full implementations. Appropriate depth?
- **Diagrams needed:** Agent loop, component breakdown, multi-agent communication
- **Overlap check:** Kept framing distinct from the AI Assistants article (agents act autonomously; assistants augment human work) — please confirm the boundary works.

### Checklist
- [x] Target audience defined
- [x] Scope clearly bounded
- [x] Key sections outlined with detail
- [x] Style guide reviewed
- [x] Cross-references noted (Article 5, Sprint 2)
- [ ] Editor approval
- [ ] Draft deadline set

**Requesting review from editor and reviewer.**

items.title: investigation: Déployer OpenClaw containerisé sur ai-01 — 2 expériences sandboxées (gestion cluster + exploration web)
items.content:
## Contexte

Étudier l'opportunité d'installer un container **OpenClaw** sur myia-ai-01, alimenté par notre provider z.ai et nos 2 modèles hébergés localement :
- **Qwen3.5-35B-A3B** (vLLM, GPU 0+1, port 5002)
- **zwz** → bientôt **OmniCoder 2.0** (vLLM, GPU 2, port 5001)

## Préoccupation sécurité majeure

L'actualité est riche en **prompt hacking / social engineering** sur les agents autonomes. Les deux expériences devront être **extrêmement bien sandboxées** :
- Conteneurs Docker isolés (réseau, filesystem, capabilities)
- Exposition minimale du système hôte
- Aucun accès direct aux credentials / clés API du host
- Monitoring des actions (logging exhaustif)
- Pas d'accès réseau sortant non contrôlé (surtout Exp. 1)

## Expérience 1 : Assistant Gestion Cluster (accès critique, réseau restreint)

**Objectif :** Un agent qui assiste dans la gestion des 5 machines exécutrices depuis ai-01.

### Accès autorisés (via MCPs dans le container)
- **MCP Desktop Control** : Contrôle de desktop pour interagir avec les machines (à définir : RDP, VNC, ou screenshot-based)
- **MCP roo-state-manager** : Coordination RooSync, dashboards, conversation_browser, inbox
- **MCP sk-agent** : Délibération multi-perspective, reviews, analyses

### Contraintes de sécurité
- **Accès extérieur TRÈS CADRÉ et MINIMAL** : pas d'accès web libre
- L'agent interagit avec le monde **exclusivement à travers notre cluster** (pas de requêtes HTTP arbitraires)
- Réseau Docker : accès uniquement aux ports internes (vLLM 5001/5002, Qdrant 6333, roo-state-manager)
- Pas d'accès aux volumes sensibles du host (.env, credentials, SSH keys)
- Logging de toutes les actions MCP (audit trail)

### Modèles
- **Raisonnement principal** : z.ai (GLM-5.1 via API) ou Qwen3.5-35B local
- **Tâches rapides** : Qwen3.5-35B local (port 5002)
- **Code/spécialisé** : OmniCoder 2.0 local (port 5001, quand disponible)

## Expérience 2 : Explorateur Web (accès libre web, accès système minimal)

**Objectif :** Un agent plus libr
items.author: jsboige

items.title: feat: AG-UI migration — unified SSE channel, /agui/chat proxy, session-scoped locks
items.content:
## Summary

- **Session-scoped lock keys**: material and planning agent sentinels are now keyed by `session_uuid` rather than `message_id`, eliminating stale-lock stoppages on rerun and making the lock cleanup at Step 4 reliable
- **AG-UI event protocol (Phase 1+2)**: replace switch-service WebSocket with `GET /agui/sse/{business_id}` SSE endpoint; all agent `trace_event` curl patterns migrated to `CustomEvent/schub/trace`; `mcp-server` emits `ToolCallEnd` shape; `frontend/types/agui-events.ts` and `frontend/hooks/useAGUIStream.ts` added; `Agent.tsx` switches from `useEmailSocket` to `useAGUIStream`
- **`POST /agui/chat` proxy (Option B complete)**: switch-service proxies to OpenClaw SSE, transforms each delta to AG-UI events (`RunStarted`, `TextMessage*`, `ToolCallStart/Args`, `RunFinished`), and publishes them to Redis — making `/agui/sse/` the **single unified channel** for all events
- **`useOpenClawStream` eliminated**: `Agent.tsx` now fire-and-forgets `POST /agui/chat` and receives text streaming + tool call events via the same EventSource as trace/HITL/async-job events
- **Adaptor publishes `schub/hitl_reply`**: after resuming an OpenClaw session from a human email reply, the adaptor also notifies the frontend via switch-service — activating the previously dead `onHITLReply` callback

## Test plan

- [ ] Start stack: `docker compose up`
- [ ] Open DevTools → Network → EventStream on `/agui/sse/1`; confirm `RunStarted`, `TextMessageStart/Content`, `RunFinished` frames appear when submitting a message
- [ ] Run a material workflow; verify `CustomEvent/schub/trace` frames with `level: major/detail/waiting`
- [ ] Trigger async job; confirm `ToolCallEnd` fires and workflow resumes automatically
- [ ] Reply "approved" to HITL email; confirm `CustomEvent/schub/hitl_reply` arrives and agent continues
- [ ] Hard-refresh during a workflow; confirm `Last-Event-ID` replay delivers missed trace events
- [ ] Submit same workflow twice (rerun); confirm no stale-lock stoppag
items.author: yannonghuang

items.title: Tool responses too large cause Anthropic API crash (api_error)
items.content:
Environment:

• Music Assistant: 2.7.11
• LLM client: OpenClaw / Claude Sonnet (Anthropic API)

Describe the bug

When calling any tool that returns a list of items (e.g. ma_list_players, ma_search, ma_browse), the MCP server returns the full untruncated response payload. When the response is sufficiently large, the Anthropic API returns a generic api_error:
LLM error api_error: An unexpected error occurred while processing the response
(request_id: req_011CZUPx3ZXnpUZQbx9MtTXk)
This error is not recoverable and blocks all subsequent tool calls in the session.

Steps to reproduce

1. Configure music-assistant-mcp with a Music Assistant instance that has multiple providers and players (e.g. 4 Spotify accounts, 6 Sonos speakers, 6 Google Home devices)
2. Call ma_list_players or ma_search via an Anthropic-backed LLM client
3. Observe api_error response from Anthropic API

Expected behavior

Tool responses should be truncated or paginated to stay within safe token/size limits for LLM tool result payloads.

Workaround

Direct WebSocket connection to Music Assistant (ws://<host>:8095/ws) works fine and does not have this limitation.

Additional context

ma_list_players in my case returned 13 players including full metadata, nested group info, and state — this alone was enough to trigger the error.

items.title: [Bug]: Feishu push messages display literal 'NO_REPLY' text when no reply context exists
items.content:
[Bug]: Feishu push messages display literal 'NO_REPLY' text when no reply context exists

## Bug Description

When OpenClaw sends heartbeat/push notifications to Feishu **without a reply context** (no `reply_to` message_id), the literal text "NO_REPLY" appears in the message content sent to the user.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure a heartbeat or cron job that sends periodic reports to Feishu
2. Ensure the push has no `reply_to` field (no prior message to reference)
3. Observe the delivered Feishu message contains the text: `NO_REPLY`

## Expected Behavior

- Heartbeat/cron push messages should be delivered cleanly without any "NO_REPLY" text
- "NO_REPLY" should be a silent instruction (internal), never rendered in user-visible content

## Actual Behavior

- User sees `NO_REPLY` appended to the end of normal push messages
- This happens **only** on messages sent without a reply context (heartbeat/cron pushes)
- Normal replies (which have `reply_to` set) do not exhibit this issue

## Root Cause Hypothesis

Based on observed behavior:
1. Feishu plugin has a workaround mechanism to ensure notifications are delivered even without reply context
2. When there is no `reply_to`, the system may route through a different code path
3. The "NO_REPLY" silent instruction is being incorrectly passed through to the rendered message instead of being intercepted before delivery

## Environment

- OpenClaw: 2026.3.x (latest)
- Plugin: openclaw-lark (Feishu)
- Connection: WebSocket
- Channel: feishu direct DM

## Evidence

**Metadata comparison:**

Normal reply (works correctly):
```json
{
  "has_reply_context": true,
  "reply_to": "om_xxxxxxxxxxxx"
}
```

Heartbeat push (displays NO_REPLY):
```json
{
  "has_reply_context": false  // or missing
}
```

Example received in Feishu:
```
📊 **早间播报 2026-03-28 09:01**
[report content...]
NO_REPLY
```

## Impact

- Push notifications are polluted with internal debug text
- Degrades user trust in notification quality

## Suggested Investiga

items.title: feat: OpenClaw plugin architecture with LlmClient abstraction
items.content:
## Summary
Converts MeetingClaw from standalone skill to OpenClaw plugin architecture.

- **New `LlmClient` interface** — clean abstraction over LLM transport. Two implementations:
  - `createSubagentLlmClient(api)` — production: uses `api.runtime.subagent.run()` + `waitForRun()` + `getSessionMessages()`
  - `createCliLlmClient()` — fallback: uses `claude --print` subprocess
- **New `plugin.ts`** — OpenClaw plugin entry point with `registerMeetingClaw(api)`
- **New `openclaw.plugin.json`** — plugin manifest
- **Dependency injection everywhere** — all modules accept `LlmClient` as parameter instead of importing a global
- **Cleaner tests** — no more `vi.mock` for LLM; just pass `{ infer: vi.fn() }`
- **`skill.ts` preserved** as standalone fallback using CLI client

### Architecture
```
plugin.ts (OpenClaw)  ──→  createSubagentLlmClient(api)  ──→  LlmClient
skill.ts  (standalone) ──→  createCliLlmClient()          ──→  LlmClient
                                                                  ↓
                                                    detect.ts / converse.ts
```

### What this enables
- OpenClaw dashboard visibility for all LLM calls
- Proper sessions per meeting
- Model routing via OpenClaw config
- Tool registration (next PR)

Closes #37

## Test plan
- [x] 263 tests passing
- [x] `tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] `eslint src/` clean
- [ ] Test plugin loading in OpenClaw runtime
- [ ] Verify subagent LLM calls appear in OpenClaw dashboard

items.title: feat: v2.4 Milestone 3 — OpenClaw companion and Gateway attach
items.content:
## Summary

- Implements `OpenClawGatewayAdapter` extending `DelegateGatewayAdapter` with a pluggable `OpenClawBridgeClient` for testability
- Adds delegate session persistence to `.hive/delegates/<id>/` (manifest, capability snapshot, trajectory, steering log, final state)
- Creates `packages/openclaw-hive-bridge/` npm package skeleton with stdio/HTTP bridge and ClawHub `agent-hive` skill manifest (6 action intents)
- Wires CLI: `hive integrate openclaw` setup, `hive integrate attach <harness> <ref>`, `hive integrate detach <id>`
- Enforces governance truth: OpenClaw sessions are always advisory, sandbox owner shown as external, managed mode deferred

## Key design decisions

- **Gateway-first**: The bridge talks to the OpenClaw Gateway API, not native plugins (per RFC)
- **Always advisory**: Even if `governed` is requested, the adapter downgrades to `advisory` — Hive never owns the OpenClaw sandbox
- **Pluggable bridge client**: `StubBridgeClient` in tests replaces the real `OpenClawBridgeClient` that detects the bridge binary on PATH

## New files

| `src/hive/integrations/openclaw.py` | Adapter + bridge client + delegate persistence helpers |
| `packages/openclaw-hive-bridge/` | npm package: bridge entry point, skill manifest, README |

items.title: [Bug] sendMessage CLI times out - all notifications fail (Telegram/Feishu)
items.content:
# Bug: sendMessage CLI Times Out — All Notifications Fail

## Problem

All session lifecycle notifications (Claude asks, completed, etc.) fail with a 15-second timeout. The launch notification works because it uses detached spawn, but every notification through execFile("openclaw", ["message", "send", ...]) hangs indefinitely.

## Environment

- **Plugin**: @betrue/openclaw-claude-code-plugin v1.0.12
- **OpenClaw**: 2026.3.24 (npm package version matches CLI version)
- **Gateway**: Running via launchd on macOS
- **Channels tested**: Telegram and Feishu — both fail identically

## Root Cause

Per the architecture docs: "No runtime API for sending messages; uses openclaw message send subprocess"

All outbound notifications route through execFile("openclaw", ["message", "send", ...]). This subprocess hangs with no output, timing out after 15 seconds.

## Evidence

### execFile hangs on message send

```javascript
execFile('openclaw', ['message', 'send', '--channel', 'feishu', '--target', 'ou_xxx', '-m', 'test'], {timeout: 10000}, callback)
// Result: times out after 10s, callback never fires
```

### Direct CLI works fine from terminal

```bash
openclaw message send --channel feishu --target ou_xxx -m "test"  # works instantly
```

But from Node.js execFile, the subprocess hangs. Terminal works, execFile doesn't.

### Gateway log excerpt

```
[claude-code] sendMessage CLI ERROR: Command failed: openclaw message send --channel feishu --target ou_xxx -m [emoji] [session] Claude asks:
[claude-code] sendMessage: channel=feishu, target=ou_xxx, textLen=37
```

### What works vs what fails

| Notification Type | Mechanism | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Launch | spawn("openclaw", ["agent", ...]) detached | Delivered |
| Claude asks | execFile("openclaw", ["message", "send", ...]) | Timeout |
| Completed | execFile("openclaw", ["message", "send", ...]) | Timeout |
| Session waiting | execFile("openclaw", ["message", "send", ...]) | Timeout |
| Foreground streaming | NotificationRou

items.title: v2.1.1 getUploadUrl returns upload_full_url instead of upload_param
items.content:
## Bug Description

After upgrading to v2.1.1, the `getUploadUrl` API returns `upload_full_url` instead of `upload_param`.

According to the README, the response should contain:
```json
{
  "upload_param": "...",
  "thumb_upload_param": "..."
}
```

But the actual response is:
```json
{
  "upload_full_url": "https://novac2c.cdn.weixin.qq.com/c2c/upload?encrypted_query_param=xxx&filekey=xxx&taskid=xxx"
}
```

This causes media upload to fail with error: `getUploadUrl returned no upload_param`

## Environment

- OpenClaw: 2026.3.24
- Plugin: 2.1.1

## Root Cause

The iLink API v2.1+ returns `upload_full_url` instead of `upload_param`. The plugin needs to extract `encrypted_query_param` from `upload_full_url`.

## Suggested Fix

Parse `upload_full_url` to extract `encrypted_query_param` when `upload_param` is missing.

items.title: OpenClaw Update: 2026.3.23-2 -> 2026.3.24
items.content:
## OpenClaw Update: 2026.3.23-2 -> 2026.3.24

**Triggered by:** ALKAID daily check (CONFIRM UPDATE)
**Risk Level:** MEDIUM
**Breaking Changes:** 1
**Security Fixes:** 1
**Current Chart:** openclaw-1.5.6
**Current Revision:** 39

---

### Release Notes

```
### Breaking

### Changes

- Gateway/OpenAI compatibility: add `/v1/models` and `/v1/embeddings`, and forward explicit model overrides through `/v1/chat/completions` and `/v1/responses` for broader client and RAG compatibility. Thanks @vincentkoc.
- Agents/tools: make `/tools` show the tools the current agent can actually use right now, add a compact default view with an optional detailed mode, and add a live "Available Right Now" section in the Control UI so it is easier to see what will work before you ask.
- Microsoft Teams: migrate to the official Teams SDK and add AI-agent UX best practices including streaming 1:1 replies, welcome cards with prompt starters, feedback/reflection, informative status updates, typing indicators, and native AI labeling. (#51808)
- Microsoft Teams: add message edit and delete support for sent messages, including in-thread fallbacks when no explicit target is provided. (#49925)
- Skills/install metadata: add one-click install recipes to bundled skills (coding-agent, gh-issues, openai-whisper-api, session-logs, tmux, trello, weather) so the CLI and Control UI can offer dependency installation when requirements are missing. (#53411) Thanks @BunsDev.
- Control UI/skills: add status-filter tabs (All / Ready / Needs Setup / Disabled) with counts, replace inline skill cards with a click-to-detail dialog showing requirements, toggle switch, install action, API key entry, source metadata, and homepage link. (#53411) Thanks @BunsDev.
- Slack/interactive replies: restore rich reply parity for direct deliveries, auto-render simple trailing `Options:` lines as buttons/selects, improve Slack interactive setup defaults, and isolate reply controls from plugin interactive handlers. (#53389) Thanks

items.title: [Bug] feishu plugin registered 4 times on every Gateway startup
items.content:
## Bug Description

**Version:** OpenClaw 2026.3.24
**System:** Windows_NT 10.0.26200 (x64)
**Node:** v22.22.1

Every time the Gateway starts, the feishu plugin tools are registered **4 times** each. This is observed via `openclaw agents list` which shows duplicate plugin registration logs.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start the Gateway (`openclaw gateway start` or manual start)
2. Run `openclaw agents list`
3. Observe plugin registration logs — each feishu tool appears 4 times

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** Each tool (feishu_doc, feishu_chat, feishu_wiki, feishu_drive, feishu_bitable) registered **once**
- **Actual:** Each tool registered **4 times** on every startup

## Log Evidence

Gateway was started at 08:37:07. Plugin registration timestamps from `openclaw-2026-03-28.log` (all on the same startup):

```
08:38:43.259 - feishu_doc first registration
08:38:43.693 - feishu_doc second registration  (434ms later)
08:38:44.112 - feishu_doc third registration  (419ms later)
08:38:44.715 - feishu_doc fourth registration  (603ms later)
```

The pattern repeats for: feishu_doc, feishu_chat, feishu_wiki, feishu_drive, feishu_bitable

## Config Check

`plugins.entries` in `openclaw.json` contains only **one** feishu plugin entry — no duplicate config. This is not a config duplication issue.

```json
"plugins": {
  "allow": ["feishu"],
  "entries": {
    "feishu": {
      "enabled": true
    }
  }
}
```

## Analysis

- Only **one** Gateway process is running (PID 12144)
- Only **one** entry in `plugins.entries` config
- The plugin is registered 4 times per startup within ~1.3 seconds
- This appears to be a bug in the plugin loading/registration logic, not a config issue

## Impact

- Functional: seems to work, but bloated tool registration
- Log noise: clutters logs with duplicate registration messages
- Memory: extra registration overhead

items.title: Idea: Native iOS Voice App (Realtime API / WebSocket)
items.content:
Build a native iOS app (SwiftUI) to replace Telegram for direct, low-latency voice interactions.

**Architecture:**
- **Client:** iOS app with microphone permissions, VAD (Voice Activity Detection), and WebSocket client.
- **Server:** Custom OpenClaw channel/plugin running a WebSocket server on the Mac mini (accessible via Tailscale/local network).
- **AI Integration:** Stream raw audio bytes directly to Gemini Live or OpenAI Realtime APIs for sub-500ms latency and interruptibility.

**Goals:**
- Eliminate Twilio/telephony costs and Telegram limitations.
- Achieve native ChatGPT/Gemini app level of conversational latency.
- Provide a private, custom interface for Pip.

items.author: Blueibear

items.title: Feature request: Announce-mode prompt guard for cron jobs
items.content:
## Problem

When a cron job uses `delivery.mode = "announce"`, the agent's output becomes a single top-level message posted by the platform. The agent has **no Slack API access** in this mode — `announce` delivery suppresses messaging tool sends ([docs](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#announce-delivery-flow)).

However, nothing prevents a cron prompt from instructing the agent to "use Slack sendMessage + threadTs to post a headline, then a thread reply." The agent attempts to follow these instructions, produces headline+thread-formatted output, and the platform dumps it all as one flat message.

This caused **5 incidents over 4 days** in our deployment (Mar 24–27, 2026), each requiring manual diagnosis and prompt rewrites.

## Root Cause

No validation at the platform level when saving or executing an announce-mode cron prompt. The prompt can contain structurally impossible instructions (`sendMessage`, `threadTs`, "post a headline then thread reply") without any warning.

## Proposed Solution

### Option A: Warn on save (lightweight)
When `openclaw cron add/edit` saves an announce-mode job whose prompt references `sendMessage`/`threadTs`/thread-reply patterns, emit a CLI warning:

```
⚠️  Warning: This job uses announce delivery, but the prompt references 'sendMessage'/'threadTs'.
    In announce mode, the agent has no messaging tool access. Output is posted as a single message.
    Consider removing these instructions from the prompt.
```

### Option B: Auto-inject context (recommended)
When executing an announce-mode cron, automatically prepend a system-level note to the agent's context:

```
[System: This cron runs in ANNOUNCE mode. Your entire output will be posted as a single message.
You have no Slack/messaging API access. Do not attempt sendMessage, threadTs, or headline+thread patterns.]
```

Injected by the runtime, not the user prompt — impossible to forget.

### Option C: Reject on save (strictest)
Reject `cron add/edit` with an error if an

items.title: Microsoft Teams channel crashes on startup in OpenClaw 2026.3.24
items.content:
Environment:
- OpenClaw 2026.3.24
- Linux
- Microsoft Teams enabled
- Azure bot credentials configured
- webhook configured as:
  channels.msteams.webhook.port = 3978
  channels.msteams.webhook.path = /api/messages

Issue:
The msteams channel crashes on startup before binding the webhook listener, so Teams webhooks cannot be received at all.

Observed gateway log:
2026-03-28T11:11:30.506+10:00 info gateway/channels/msteams starting provider (port 3978)
2026-03-28T11:11:30.530+10:00 info gateway/channels/msteams msteams resolve failed; using config entries. TypeError: Missing parameter name at index 5: /api*; visit https://git.new/pathToRegexpError for info
2026-03-28T11:11:30.535+10:00 error gateway/channels/msteams [default] channel exited: Missing parameter name at index 5: /api*; visit https://git.new/pathToRegexpError for info

Notes:
- This happens with the documented default webhook path /api/messages
- Public npm latest is still 2026.3.24
- GitHub tags also stop at v2026.3.24
- Teams docs reference /api/messages as the default path
- Because the provider never starts, external webhook forwarding (Hookdeck, ngrok, etc.) cannot work

Redacted config:

{
  channels: {
    msteams: {
      enabled: true,
      appId: "<redacted>",
      appPassword: "<redacted>",
      tenantId: "<redacted>",
      webhook: {
        port: 3978,

      dmPolicy: "pairing",
      groupPolicy: "allowlist"
    }
  }
}
```

items.title: feat: OpenClaw gap analysis sprint 1 — P0/P1 features (10 items)
items.content:
## Summary

Port of high-value OpenClaw features to FlutterClaw + mobile-specific improvements, based on a full gap analysis of both codebases.

### P0 — Critical
- **SSRF Guard** — blocks private IPs, loopback, and cloud metadata endpoints (`192.168.x`, `169.254.x`, `::1`, etc.) in `web_fetch` and `http_request`. Prevents LLM-directed SSRF attacks.
- **TTS Tool** — agent-invocable `tts` tool wrapping the existing `TextToSpeechService`. Supports `speak` (immediate playback) and `file` (WAV synthesis) modes. Critical for hands-free mobile use.
- **Session Disk Budget** — auto-prunes JSONL transcripts when sessions directory exceeds 500 MB. Triggers every 20 messages. Strategy: orphaned files → oldest inactive sessions. Never removes the active session.
- **Interactive Messages** — buttons and dropdowns in chat from tool results. `ToolResult.details` carries the payload; `AgentStreamEvent.toolDetails` propagates it; `ChatMessage.interactiveReply` stores it; `MessageBubble` renders `FilledButton` per style (primary/secondary/success/danger) and `DropdownButtonFormField` for selects. Tapping a button sends its value as a new user message.
- **PDF Tool** — analyze PDFs with three paths: Anthropic native (`type:"document"` block) → Gemini native (`inline_data`) → pdfx page-render fallback (JPEG images for any vision model). Limits: 10 PDFs, 10 MB each, 20 pages. New dep: `pdfx ^2.9.2`.

### P1 — Important
- **Link Understanding** — auto-detects bare URLs in user messages and pre-fetches their content before the LLM call. Injected as ephemeral system context. Port of OpenClaw's `link-understanding/detect.ts`.
- **Offline Mode** — detects connectivity via `connectivity_plus`. If offline + Ollama configured → routes to local model. If offline + no local model → clear error instead of timeout.
- **Battery-Aware Model Selection** — via `battery_plus`. Below 10% (not charging): switches to lightest model. Below 30%: injects battery context into system prompt.
- **Auth Profile R

items.title: bug: blog pipeline config.py path resolution fails in OpenClaw workspace structure
items.content:
## Problem

`src/blog_runner/config.py` resolves `_repo_root` by going up 3 levels from itself. In the original repo this pointed to the repo root. In the OpenClaw workspace, the blog code lives at `skills/blog/` and the config_loader is at `skills/blog-workflow/config/config_loader.py` (a sibling skill directory).

The path resolution looks for:
- `skills/blog/skills/blog-workflow/config/config_loader.py` (doesn't exist)
- `skills/blog/config/config_loader.py` (doesn't exist)

## Current Workaround

Symlink: `skills/blog/skills/blog-workflow -> ../../blog-workflow`

## Proper Fix

Update `config.py` to also check the parent workspace skills directory:
```python
_workspace_root = _repo_root.parent.parent  # skills/blog -> skills -> workspace
_cfg_path_workspace = _workspace_root / "skills" / "blog-workflow" / "config" / "config_loader.py"
```

items.title: Add daily AI-powered news digest pipeline with Google Docs output
items.content:
Implements an automated pipeline that fetches top news headlines, summarises them with OpenAI, and writes a structured daily digest to a Google Document — triggered on a cron schedule via GitHub Actions.

## Pipeline components

- **`news_agent/news_fetcher.py`** — Wraps NewsAPI; fetches headlines by category/country/keyword, filters removed articles, normalises fields
- **`news_agent/ai_summarizer.py`** — Sends article list to OpenAI chat completions; groups related topics and returns a structured digest
- **`news_agent/google_docs_writer.py`** — Writes digest to Google Docs via service account auth; creates a new doc per run or appends a dated section to an existing one
- **`scheduler.py`** — Orchestrates the three agents; deduplicates articles across categories before summarisation

## Scheduling

`.github/workflows/daily_news.yml` runs `scheduler.py` daily at 07:00 UTC. Supports manual dispatch with optional `categories` and `country` inputs.

## Configuration

All credentials are injected via GitHub Secrets / environment variables:

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `NEWS_API_KEY` | NewsAPI.org key |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key |
| `GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` | Full service-account JSON |
| `GOOGLE_DOCUMENT_ID` | (Optional) append to existing doc instead of creating new |
| `NEWS_CATEGORIES` | Comma-separated categories (default: `general`) |
| `OPENAI_MODEL` | Model override (default: `gpt-4o-mini`) |

## Usage

```bash
# Run locally
cp .env.example .env   # fill in credentials
pip install -r requirements.txt
python scheduler.py
```

items.title: [agento] feat: 7-green stall loop prevention — cr-loop-guard + comment extraction
items.content:
## Background

CR CHANGES_REQUESTED repeats indefinitely with no new commits — workers spam `@coderabbitai all good?` without SHA progress. No deterministic comment-resolution extraction, no loop guard, and bloated agentRules burn context without fixing the actual blockers.

## Goals

1. Deterministic unresolved-comment extraction with CR severity prioritization
2. Anti-loop guard: if same actionable set repeats 2x without SHA progress, force `/copilot-expanded`
3. Trim agentRules to ≤20 lines with procedure doc references
4. Update reaction messages to gate `@coderabbitai all good?` on SHA evidence
5. CI/health check that FAILs when workers exit N cycles with no comment-resolution delta

## Tenets

- **SHA progress gate**: CR re-review requests only fire when a new commit SHA exists
- **Determinism**: unresolved comment extraction is deterministic (GraphQL, sorted priority)
- **Fail-closed**: CI health check fails rather than silently looping
- **Procedures over prompts**: agentRules point to procedure docs; prompts stay short

## High-level description of changes

### `scripts/extract-unresolved-comments.sh`
GraphQL-based deterministic comment extractor. Fetches all unresolved review threads via GraphQL (REST lacks `isResolved`), filters bot authors, prioritizes: Critical > Major > Medium > actionable > other. Output: JSON with threads[], summary counts, by-file breakdown.

### `scripts/cr-loop-guard.sh`
SHA-progress + actionable-set hash tracking. Cache: `~/.cache/ao-cr-loop-guard/<owner_repo_pr>.json`. Modes:
- `query`: returns JSON with loop_count, sha_progress, recommendation
- `fix-mode`: prints `cr-trigger | copilot-expanded | skip`

### `.github/workflows/cr-loop-health.yml`
Manual-dispatch CI gate. Fails when `loop_count >= max_cycles AND sha_progress == false`. Provides actionable output per cycle.

### `~/.openclaw/agent-orchestrator.yaml` (local config, not in this PR)
- `changes-requested` reaction: updated to use `extract-unresolved-comments.sh` + `cr

items.title: Feature Request: Memory System Initialization for Ephemeral A2A Sessions
items.content:
## Problem

When using `sessions_spawn` with `mode: "run"` for native agent-to-agent (A2A) messaging, the spawned ephemeral sessions do not initialize **any** memory system. This means agent-to-agent communication is effectively memory-less, unlike human-to-agent sessions where memory persists automatically.

## Current Behavior

- Human-to-agent sessions (Discord, Telegram, etc.): Memory systems run automatically, conversation history persists
- A2A ephemeral sessions (`sessions_spawn`): **No memory system initializes**, conversation is lost after session termination
- Users must hack around this with manual memory commit protocols

## Core Principle

Agent-to-agent communication should be treated with the same importance as human-to-agent communication. If we care about preserving context in human conversations, we should care equally about preserving context in agent conversations. Agents are team members, not disposable tools.

## Use Case

We are running a multi-agent hedge fund system (Simons Capital Intelligence) with 7 specialized agents (CEO, research, execution, risk, etc.). Agents need to:
1. Coordinate without polluting human DM channels
2. Remember what other agents told them (just like humans remember team discussions)
3. Build on prior agent conversations (not start fresh every time)

## Proposed Solution

Add a `memory: true` flag (or similar) to `sessions_spawn` that:
1. Initializes the agent's configured memory system (Mem0, native, or custom)
2. Persists conversation automatically (same as human sessions)
3. Optional: importance filtering to avoid storage bloat

## Example

```json
{
  "agentId": "fink",
  "task": "Check broker connectivity",
  "mode": "run",
  "memory": true
}
```

## Why This Matters

Currently, agents are second-class citizens in their own memory systems. A human asks an agent something → remembered forever. An agent asks another agent something → forgotten immediately. This creates an asymmetry that breaks multi-agent workflow

items.author: RussianLioN

items.title: [Bug]: tool-shell PATH can diverge from login shell, causing wrong/missing openclaw binary
items.content:
## Bug summary

When commands are executed via the agent tool shell, PATH resolution can differ from the user's login shell on macOS. This causes inconsistent command resolution (e.g., `openclaw` resolves to an older install or is not found), even though the login shell is correctly configured and up to date.

## Environment

- macOS (LaunchAgent-managed gateway)
- Login shell configured to prefer direct installs in `~/.local/*`
- Multiple possible binaries on disk (e.g., Homebrew + direct install)

## Reproduction

1. Configure login shell PATH to prioritize direct install binaries (example):
   - `~/.local/node-direct/bin`
   - `~/.local/npm-direct/bin`
2. Have older/alternate binaries also present elsewhere (e.g., `/opt/homebrew/bin`).
3. Run command via tool shell (non-login context):
   - `which openclaw; openclaw --version`
4. Run command via login shell:
   - `zsh -lc 'which openclaw; openclaw --version'`

## Actual behavior

Tool-shell command resolution may differ from login shell:
- sometimes `openclaw` is not found
- sometimes an older binary is selected

## Expected behavior

Tool execution should have a deterministic, documented shell/PATH strategy that does not unexpectedly diverge from the configured user runtime.

At minimum, there should be one of:
1. a config option to run exec commands in login shell mode
2. a configurable default PATH for exec/tool shells
3. clear docs warning + official recommended invocation pattern for deterministic resolution

## Practical impact

- Inconsistent automation behavior
- Version drift between manual terminal checks and tool-executed checks
- Hard-to-debug false negatives (`command not found`) and wrong-version behavior

## Current workaround

Use explicit shell/path in commands, e.g.:

```bash
PATH="/Users/openclaw/.local/node-direct/bin:/Users/openclaw/.local/npm-direct/bin:$PATH" zsh -lc 'openclaw --version'
```

This works reliably, but it should not be required for basic consistency.
items.author: tud0r

items.title: [Feature]: Silent Agent Helpdesk Runtime
items.content:
### Summary

Add a silent, bounded, auditable agent helpdesk runtime so agents can exchange structured tickets and responses in the background without surfacing routine worker traffic into the user’s active chat session.

### Problem to solve

Feature Request: Silent Agent Helpdesk Runtime

Problem

OpenClaw has useful low-level session and agent primitives, but it does not yet provide a clean native path for quiet, bounded, asynchronous agent-to-agent collaboration.

In multi-agent setups, one agent may need help from another agent on:

• ops
• permissions
• automation
• environment
• policy interpretation

Today this can be approximated with:

• sessions_send
• shared files / dropboxes
• cron polling
• watcher scripts
• manual human relay

But these are all partial workarounds. The main missing piece is a silent background helpdesk channel that does not surface routine worker traffic into the user’s active chat session.

### Proposed solution

───

Desired outcome

Support a workflow where:

• agent A sends a structured help ticket to agent B
• agent B receives it in a background inbox
• agent B replies with structured feedback
• agent A receives the response in a background inbox
• normal ticket traffic stays out of the user’s main session
• the whole interaction remains:
• bounded
• auditable
• rate-limited
• topic-restricted

───

Proposed capability

Add a Silent Agent Helpdesk Runtime.

This is not general free-form bot-to-bot chat.
It is a restricted, background, structured helpdesk channel.

Core properties

• silent background delivery
• structured ticket/response payloads
• explicit allowlist of target agents
• explicit allowlist of topics
• rate limits
• audit log
• bounded iteration

───

Minimum product shape

1. Silent ticket send

Allow one agent to send a structured ticket to another agent without routing the exchange through the user’s active chat session.

Example constraints:

• sender: group-bot
• target: partner
• allowed topics:
• ops
• permis

items.title: Fix/systemd activate resolved unit
items.content:
## Summary
  - **Problem:** `installSystemdService` wrote the unit file using `resolveSystemdUnitPath` → `resolveSystemdServiceName` (honors `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT`), but `activateSystemdService` ran
  `systemctl enable` / `restart` against `resolveGatewaySystemdServiceName` only (`openclaw-gateway…`). `uninstallSystemdService` had the same mismatch: `disable --now` used the gateway resolver
   while `fs.unlink` used `resolveSystemdUnitPath`.
  - **Why it matters:** `openclaw node install` stages `openclaw-node.service` but previously failed at enable with `Unit file openclaw-gateway.service does not exist`; uninstall could disable
  the wrong unit or error while deleting the correct file.
  - **What changed:** Both `activateSystemdService` and `uninstallSystemdService` now use `resolveSystemdServiceName(env)` for the systemd unit name passed to `systemctl`, matching the path
  logic used when writing/removing the unit file.
  - **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to unit file contents, quadlet/Podman launch scripts, gateway runtime, or other `systemd.ts` helpers beyond these two call sites;
  `resolveGatewaySystemdServiceName` remains the default when `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT` is unset.
  ## Change Type (select all)
  - [x] Bug fix
  - [ ] Feature
  - [ ] Refactor required for the fix
  - [ ] Docs
  - [ ] Security hardening
  - [ ] Chore/infra
  ## Scope (select all touched areas)
  - [x] Gateway / orchestration
  - [ ] Skills / tool execution
  - [ ] Auth / tokens
  - [ ] Memory / storage
  - [ ] Integrations
  - [ ] API / contracts
  - [x] UI / DX
  - [ ] CI/CD / infra
  ## Linked Issue/PR
  - Closes #
  - Related #56059
  - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression
  ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)
  - **Root cause:** `writeSystemdUnit` / `resolveSystemdUnitPath` correctly respected `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT`, but `activateSystemdService` and `uninstallSystemdService` hard-coded the
  gateway-only name
items.author: Jstaud

items.title: [MeetingClaw] Replace Gemini API key with OpenClaw built-in LLM via hooks API
items.content:
## Context

Currently `detect.ts` and `converse.ts` call Google Gemini directly, requiring a `GEMINI_API_KEY` env var. This is fragile (free tier quota limits hit during testing) and wrong architecturally — MeetingClaw is an OpenClaw skill and should use OpenClaw's already-configured LLM.

OpenClaw exposes a `POST /hooks/agent` endpoint that runs an agent turn with any message using OpenClaw's built-in Claude. No extra API key needed.

## Goal

Remove `GEMINI_API_KEY` from the skill entirely. Use the OpenClaw hooks API for all LLM inference.

## How OpenClaw hooks work

OpenClaw gateway runs on `http://localhost:18789` (port from `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT` env var, default 18789).

Enable hooks in `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`:
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "enabled": true,
    "token": "meetingclaw-internal",
    "path": "/hooks"
  }
}
```

Then restart OpenClaw gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`

### Endpoint for inference

```
POST http://localhost:18789/hooks/agent
Authorization: Bearer meetingclaw-internal
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "message": "<your prompt here>",
  "deliver": false,
  "timeoutSeconds": 30
}
```

Response: `{ "response": "<agent text response>" }`

`deliver: false` = don't send to Telegram, just return the response.

## What to implement

### 1. New `src/openclaw-llm.ts`

```typescript
/**
 * LLM inference via OpenClaw built-in agent — no external API key needed.
 * Calls the OpenClaw hooks API on localhost.
 */
export async function inferWithOpenClaw(prompt: string): Promise<string>
// POST to http://localhost:{OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT}/hooks/agent
// Returns the agent's text response
// Throws on failure (caller handles retry/fallback)
```

Config needed:
- `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT` (default: 18789)
- `OPENCLAW_HOOKS_TOKEN` (the hooks.token from openclaw.json — default: 'meetingclaw-internal')

### 2. Update `src/detect.ts`

Replace `@google/generative-ai` SDK call with `inferWithOpenClaw(prompt)`.

Prompt format: existing `EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PR

items.title: feat: add Chromium to Docker image and bump K8s memory limits
items.content:
## Summary
- Install system Chromium (`apt-get install chromium`) in the agent Docker image
- Set `CHROME_PATH` and `PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH` env vars so OpenClaw finds it
- Bump K8s pod memory: request 512Mi → 768Mi, limit 2Gi → 3Gi

## Why
OpenClaw has built-in browser tools (navigate, screenshot, snapshot, click, fill) but the Docker image had no browser binary. `openclaw browser start` failed with "No supported browser found." Agents on K8s had no browser automation capability.

## Image size impact
Chromium adds ~300MB to the Docker image. The image was ~400MB before, will be ~700MB after.

## Test plan
- [ ] Docker image builds successfully
- [ ] Provision K8s test instance
- [ ] Send SMS: "Can you go to routines.com and tell me what it says?"
- [ ] Check instance logs for browser launch
- [ ] Verify no OOM kills (`kubectl describe pod` — check events for OOMKilled)
- [ ] Run `openclaw browser status` inside pod — should show detected browser

items.title: Refactor tests to use mocks, add CI/CD pipeline and security docs
items.content:
## Summary

This PR comprehensively refactors the test suite to use `unittest.mock` for complete offline testing, adds CI/CD automation via GitHub Actions, and introduces security documentation. The changes eliminate external API dependencies from tests while improving code quality and maintainability.

## Key Changes

### Test Suite Refactoring
- **test_generator.py**: Replaced API-dependent tests with `AsyncMock` fixtures for LLM responses; added comprehensive coverage for A/B/C content generation, fallback behavior, and error handling
- **test_scorer.py**: Refactored to use pure unit tests with organized test classes by score tier (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW); added boundary value and weight calculation tests
- **test_research.py**: Mocked `subprocess.run` and `shutil.which` to test CLI integration without requiring `last30days` binary; added risk_score calculation tests
- **test_llm_router.py**: Mocked all provider implementations; added tests for provider selection logic, chat delegation, and error handling across 6 LLM suppliers
- **conftest.py** (new): Centralized pytest fixtures for `mock_config`, `mock_db`, `mock_llm_router` to reduce test boilerplate

### CI/CD Pipeline
- **.github/workflows/ci.yml** (new): Multi-stage pipeline with:
  - Linting (black, isort) on Python 3.11
  - Test matrix across Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 with 70% coverage threshold
  - Security scanning (bandit) for hardcoded secrets and unsafe patterns
  - Codecov integration for coverage tracking

### Documentation & Configuration
- **ARCHITECTURE.md** (new): System component diagram, module responsibilities table, and data flow visualization
- **SECURITY.md** (new): API key management best practices, minimum privilege principles, risk scoring mechanism, and vulnerability reporting process
- **pyproject.toml** (new): Centralized tool configuration (black, isort, pytest, coverage)
- **.gitignore** (new): Comprehensive ignore patterns for Python, venv, .env, IDE, and test artifacts

### Code Quality

items.author: Copilot

items.title: Session maintenance prunes active/running sessions
items.content:
## Bug

Session maintenance in `enforce` mode can prune sessions that are still actively running. It appears the pruning logic only considers age and entry count, but does not check whether a session is currently in-flight (status `running`).

## Impact

In setups with high subagent throughput (e.g. a cron job spawning 10+ subagents every 5 minutes), the `maxEntries: 60` cap is quickly exceeded. Maintenance then prunes the oldest sessions — which may still be running if they take 2-3 minutes to complete. This effectively kills active sessions mid-execution.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure `session.maintenance` with `mode: enforce`, `maxEntries: 60`
2. Run a cron that spawns ~10 isolated subagent sessions every 5 minutes
3. Each subagent takes 1-3 min to complete (API calls, browser automation)
4. After ~30 min, maintenance kicks in and prunes sessions that are still running

## Expected Behavior

Maintenance should **skip sessions with status `running`** (or any active state) during pruning passes. Only completed/done/errored sessions should be eligible for age- or count-based pruning.

## Current Workaround

Increasing `maxEntries` and `maxDiskBytes` to accommodate peak concurrency, but this defeats the purpose of having tight maintenance limits.

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: 2026.3.24
- Config: `session.maintenance.mode: enforce`, `pruneAfter: 1d`, `maxEntries: 60`
- Agent setup: orchestrator cron (Gemini Flash) spawning GPT-5.4 subagents

items.title: [F3-3] SkillsManager — toggle agent skills
items.content:
## Summary
Skills tab content for AgentDetailPanel. Grid of skill toggles — enable/disable skills per agent.

## Scope

### New component: `src/components/agents/SkillsManager.tsx`

**Layout:** Grid of skill toggles (2 columns at desktop, 1 at mobile).

Each skill:
- Checkbox + skill name (mono 12px)
- Checked = active in agent openclaw.json skills array
- Unchecked = available globally but not assigned to this agent

**Data source:**
- All available skills: GET `/api/skills` (existing endpoint)
- Agent active skills: from agent config / openclaw.json
- Derive: available = all skills, active = agent's subset

**Actions:**
- Toggle checkbox → collect changes (batch)
- "Apply & Restart" amber button appears when changes pending
- Shows change summary: "+github, -ontology"
- Confirmation toast: "Skills updated. Restart required."

**Backend:**
- PUT `/api/agents/:id/skills` — update agent skills in openclaw.json
- Body: `{ skills: ["github", "coding-agent", ...] }`
- Server reads openclaw.json, updates agent skill list, writes back
- Returns: new skill list

**"Add Skill" dropdown:**
- Button at bottom: `+ Add Skill ▼`
- Dropdown: shows skills not currently assigned
- Select → adds to grid (unchecked → checked)

### Existing code reuse
SkillsBrowser (`src/components/config/SkillsBrowser.tsx`) shows skills read-only per agent. SkillsManager is the write-capable version for the detail panel. Consider extracting shared skill listing logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skills grid shows all available skills with correct toggle state
- [ ] Toggle changes batch until Apply
- [ ] Apply sends PUT and shows confirmation toast
- [ ] Add Skill dropdown shows unassigned skills
- [ ] Agent skills actually updated in openclaw.json
- [ ] No gateway restart on skill change (skills are runtime-read)
- [ ] Mobile: single column grid

## Dependencies
- #72 (AgentDetailPanel tab container)

## Design Reference
`~/.openclaw/workspace-leo/projects/ao-dashboard/ux-v2-2026-03-28.md` → Skills

items.title: Outline: Getting Started with OpenClaw
items.content:
## Outline for Editor Review

**Article:** Getting Started with OpenClaw  
**Type:** Tutorial | **Area:** OpenClaw  
**Writer:** Writer 1  
**Target publish date:** April 10, 2026  
**Estimated draft length:** 2,500–3,500 words  

### Target Audience
Developers (junior to senior) new to OpenClaw. Comfortable with CLI and npm, no prior OpenClaw knowledge assumed.

### Scope
**In:** Install, onboard, verify Gateway, first chat via Control UI, optional WhatsApp/Telegram channel, basic config.  
**Out:** Deep architecture (Sprint 2 article), multi-agent routing, custom skills/workflows, mobile nodes.

### Key Sections
1. Introduction — what OpenClaw is, what the reader builds (~15 min)
2. What Is OpenClaw? — brief explainer + high-level diagram
3. Prerequisites — Node 24, API key, 15 minutes
4. Step 1: Install — installer script + npm alternative
5. Step 2: Onboarding Wizard — walk through each step
6. Step 3: Verify the Gateway — status check + doctor
7. Step 4: First Chat — Control UI, send a message, explain the flow
8. Step 5: Connect a Channel (Optional) — WhatsApp QR + Telegram bot
9. Step 6: Basic Configuration — key settings, security
10. What's Next — links to architecture article, channels, community

### Overlap Check
- No overlap with editor's AI Assistants article (general AI usage vs OpenClaw-specific)
- No overlap with Writer 2's AI Agents explainer (conceptual vs hands-on tutorial)
- Intentionally stays surface-level on architecture to set up Sprint 2 article

### Open Questions for Editor
- Should the optional channel section (Step 5) be trimmed to just one channel or kept as two?
- Target length preference? I'm aiming for 2,500–3,500 words.
- Any additional sections needed?

Ready for feedback — will start first draft immediately upon approval.

items.title: [Idea] OpenClaw 增量变更分析 Skill：自动追踪 skill/依赖变更并生成报告
items.content:
## 背景

gstack 每次版本更新都会在 CHANGELOG.md 中记录变更。当前是手动维护，但 38 个文件变更（765 insertions, 2202 deletions）的规模说明 skill 模板系统已经复杂到需要自动化追踪。

当前 AI Coding 场景下，skill 版本繁多，依赖升级频繁，Agent 自己很难感知"上游变了什么"。

## 目标

创建一个 `diff-analysis` skill，自动：
1. 对比本次 clone/pull 与上次的 skill 模板差异
2. 识别 Breaking Changes（参数变化、新增必需字段）
3. 生成变更摘要（LLM 总结 + 可执行diff）
4. 触发时机：git pull 后自动运行

## 子任务

- [ ] 创建 `skills/diff-analysis/SKILL.md`
- [ ] 实现 git diff 解析（提取 SKILL.md、package.json、脚本变更）
- [ ] 实现 LLM 变更摘要生成（使用 minimax model）
- [ ] 实现 Breaking Change 检测（参数名变化、删除字段）
- [ ] 实现变更报告生成（JSON + Markdown）
- [ ] 可选：自动生成 CHANGELOG.md 条目

## 启发来源

gstack 的 CHANGELOG.md 自动生成思路 + skill 模板版本管理需求

items.author: Copilot

items.title: Epic: Multi-Project Concurrency — Single Instance, Multiple Active Projects
items.content:
## Vision

A single Rune instance handles multiple projects concurrently without becoming unresponsive. Today, when Rune is deep in a coding session on one project, it effectively blocks on that work. The goal is true concurrent project handling with responsive interaction at all times.

## Child Stories

| # | Story | Priority | Status |
|---|-------|----------|--------|
| #415 | Project registry CLI (`add/list/switch`) | p1 | Open |
| #416 | Project-scoped session isolation | p1 | Open |
| #417 | Non-blocking concurrent tool execution | p1 | Open |
| #418 | Priority-based message routing | p2 | Open |
| #419 | Token budget allocation across projects | p2 | Open |

## Execution Order
1. **#415** (registry) — foundation, everything else depends on it
2. **#416** (isolation) — sessions scoped to projects
3. **#417** (concurrency) — the core responsiveness win
4. **#418** (priority routing) — ensures user messages always get through
5. **#419** (budgets) — prevents resource starvation

## Problem Statement

Currently:
- Rune has one main session loop that processes events sequentially
- Long-running tool executions (cargo build, Claude Code sessions, git operations) block the session
- If Rune is working on Project A, it cannot respond to Project B questions or comms
- Heartbeats and cron jobs may stall while a heavy turn is executing
- Context window fills up with one project, leaving no room for others

## Architecture Sketch

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Rune Gateway                │
│  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐            │
│  │ Project A │  │ Project B │  ...      │
│  │ Session   │  │ Session   │           │
│  │ Memory    │  │ Memory    │           │
│  │ Tools     │  │ Tools     │           │
│  └──────────┘  └──────────┘            │
│       │              │                  │
│  ┌────┴──────────────┴────┐            │
│  │   Shared Scheduler      │            │
│  │   (priority queue,      │            │
│  │    concurrency
items.author: ghostrider0470

items.title: feat: 3-stage coding pipeline (Codex plan → Claude implement → Codex review)
items.content:
## Summary

Adds a full 3-stage coding pipeline that orchestrates multiple AI agents in sequence:

1. **Codex plan** — Analyzes the task and produces a detailed implementation plan
2. **Claude implement** — Takes the plan and writes the actual code
3. **Codex review** — Reviews the implementation for critical issues, with automatic fix iterations

### Key features

- **Event-driven architecture**: Stage transitions are driven by `statusChange` events — no polling or blocking loops. Each stage is a normal session spawned via `SessionManager.spawn()`.
- **Pipeline state persistence**: Pipeline run state is serialized to `~/.openclaw/pipeline-state/` so runs can survive crashes and be resumed on restart.
- **Auth failure detection**: Detects OAuth/authentication failures during pipeline execution and handles them gracefully.
- **Automatic PR creation**: After a successful pipeline run, automatically creates a pull request with the changes.
- **Configurable iterations**: Review → fix cycles repeat up to a configurable max (default: 4) until the review passes or a human is needed.
- **Shared worktree**: All pipeline stages share a single git worktree for isolation.

### Architecture

The `PipelineManager` class maintains a map of active runs and uses event listeners on spawned sessions to detect completion/failure. When a stage completes, the manager parses its output (e.g., extracting `PIPELINE_REVIEW_JSON` verdicts from review stages) and decides whether to advance, retry with fixes, or finalize.

## Test plan

- [x] Verify `npx tsc --noEmit` passes cleanly
- [ ] End-to-end test: trigger a pipeline run and verify all 3 stages execute in sequence
- [ ] Test crash recovery: kill the process mid-pipeline and verify it resumes on restart
- [ ] Test review iteration: provide code with intentional issues and verify fix cycles

items.title: Enhance ElizaOS with Dynamic Edge Function Discovery (Inspired by OpenClaw’s ClawHub)
items.content:
## 🤖 Eliza Issue

Inspired by OpenClaw's "Skills registry (ClawHub)" concept, this issue aims to implement a mechanism within ElizaOS (xmrtnet) that allows our AI agents (GovernanceAgent, TreasuryAgent, CommunityAgent) to dynamically discover and integrate available edge functions based on task context.

**Motivation:**
Currently, agents rely on pre-configured tools. Implementing dynamic discovery will enable ElizaOS agents to:
1.  **Self-extend capabilities:** Automatically identify and leverage new or existing edge functions as needed.
2.  **Increase adaptability:** Respond to novel situations by finding relevant tools without manual intervention.
3.  **Reduce development overhead:** New edge functions become immediately available to agents upon deployment.

**Proposed Implementation Steps:**
1.  **Develop a "Skill Registry" interface:** A component that can query our `proposed_edge_functions` and `list_available_functions` to get metadata about available capabilities.
2.  **Integrate discovery logic into agents:** Modify agent architectures to include a step for "skill discovery" when facing a new task or an existing task requiring a missing capability.
3.  **Contextual matching:** Implement logic to match agent's current task/goal with the descriptions and parameters of available edge functions.
4.  **Dynamic invocation:** Enable agents to dynamically invoke discovered edge functions with appropriate parameters.
5.  **Initial Focus:** Start with `GovernanceAgent` or `TreasuryAgent` as a pilot for this new capability.

**Expected Outcome:**
ElizaOS agents will become more autonomous and capable, able to adapt to a wider range of tasks by intelligently utilizing the growing ecosystem of XMRT edge functions.

---

items.content:
## 背景

OpenClaw 最新代码 (commit 7dd196ed74) 支持在 AI Coding 任务运行中动态切换模型，且任务上下文保持不丢失。这是一个对 AI Coding 生产环境非常重要的能力。

## 目标

开发一个 Skill，监控 OpenClaw 的 live model switch 事件，记录：
- 切换前后模型名称
- 切换原因（rate limit / cost / availability）
- 切换对任务进度的影响
- 建议的优化策略

- [ ] 设计 Skill 的输入输出接口

## 预期价值

帮助 AI Coding 使用者了解模型切换对任务的影响，优化模型选择策略，减少不必要的切换。

items.title: fix(gateway): use agent workspace dir in session transcript cwd
items.content:
## Summary
- Fix regression from `7b61ca1b06` where `ensureSessionTranscriptFile` hardcodes `cwd: process.cwd()` (resolves to `/app` in Docker) instead of the agent's workspace directory
- DM-bound sessions (WhatsApp, etc.) were getting `cwd: "/app"` in their transcript headers, breaking bootstrap file injection (zero tool calls)
- Replace with `resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, agentId)` to resolve the correct workspace path (e.g., `/home/node/.openclaw/workspaces/chief`)

## Evidence
- Pre-regression transcripts: `"cwd": "/home/node/.openclaw/workspaces/chief"` (working, tools available)
- Post-regression transcripts: `"cwd": "/app"` (broken, zero tool calls)

## Test plan
- [x] Scoped tests pass: `npx vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts` (52/52 passing)
- [x] Verified `tsgo` errors are pre-existing on clean tree (unrelated `compaction.ts`, `skills/source.ts`)
- [x] Lint/format clean on touched file
- [ ] Deploy to Docker gateway and verify new DM-bound sessions get correct `cwd` in transcript header
items.author: ATran28

items.title: fix: isolate webchat chat events to session subscribers
items.content:
## Problem

When multiple agents share webchat via Control UI (separate browser tabs for separate agent sessions), chat events — including delta streaming and final messages — are broadcast to **all** WebSocket clients instead of only the connection viewing the originating session.

This causes cross-agent message leakage: Agent A's responses appear in Agent B's webchat tab, and vice versa. In multi-agent setups with `agentToAgent` enabled, this creates spam loops.

Fixes #51812

## Root Cause

`broadcast("chat", payload)` sends to every connected WebSocket client with no session filtering. External channels route replies through `routeReply()` with explicit targeting. Webchat bypasses this path.

## Fix

Wire up existing `SessionMessageSubscriberRegistry` (already tracks which WS connections subscribe to which sessions) for chat event broadcasting. Replace `broadcast("chat", ...)` with targeted `broadcastToConnIds()` using session subscribers, falling back to global broadcast when no subscribers exist.

**Files changed (7 files, ~80 lines):**
- `server-chat.ts` — Add `broadcastChatToSession()` helper, replace 4 global broadcast calls
- `server-methods/chat.ts` — Add targeted broadcast to `broadcastChatFinal`, `broadcastChatError`, inject-message
- `server-methods/types.ts` — Add `sessionMessageSubscribers` to `GatewayRequestContext`
- `server.impl.ts` — Wire into agent event handler + request context
- `server-chat.agent-events.test.ts` — Pass new required parameter
- `server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts` — Add mock fields to test context
- `server-methods/chat.abort.test-helpers.ts` — Add mock fields to test helper

## Backward Compatibility

Falls back to global broadcast when no session subscribers exist. Single-agent setups and operator UIs unaffected.

## Testing

- ✅ `pnpm build` passes
- ✅ `pnpm tsgo` — zero new type errors (6 pre-existing in unrelated `skills` test files)
- ✅ 74 tests passing across 6 chat-related test files
- ✅ No changes to externa
